title: quercus faginea subsp. broteroi
intro: Quercus faginea subsp. broteroi is a subspecies of the Pyrenean oak tree, a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Quercus faginea subsp. broteroi include reducing fever, treating wounds, and aiding in the management of gastrointestinal issues. The bark and leaves can be prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these herbal preparations.
